hero.title: 6 Days in Nepal.
hero.intro: The classic Nepal circuit, perfectly paced. Ancient temples and watching-eye stupas in Kathmandu, a Himalayan sunrise from Nagarkot's ridge, and the still blue water of Phewa Lake with Machapuchare reflected in it. Nepal's greatest hits, nothing missed.
hero.heroImageAlt: Phewa Lake Pokhara Nepal with Machapuchare Fishtail Mountain

includes: Domestic Flight: Pokhara–Kathmandu or Road Transfer
excludes: Nepal Visa on Arrival (USD30 for 15 days)
Entrance Fees to temples and sites

overview.heading: 6 Days Best of Nepal: The Full Picture
body: This is the Nepal package that covers the country's three defining experiences: the ancient civilisation of Kathmandu, the Himalayan panorama of Nagarkot, and the serene lakeside beauty of Pokhara. Six days is enough to experience each properly, without anything feeling rushed.

Most Nepal first-timers choose this exact combination, and it is easy to see why. The cultural weight of Kathmandu's UNESCO temples, the mountain drama of a Nagarkot sunrise, and the still perfection of Phewa Lake with the Fishtail Mountain reflected in it — these are three experiences that stay with you permanently.

highlights.heading: Why This Is Our Most Popular Nepal Package

items.title: Kathmandu's UNESCO Temple Circuit
items.body: Two full days in Kathmandu — the sacred ghats of Pashupatinath, the watching eyes of Boudhanath, Swayambhunath's hilltop stupas and the medieval squares of Bhaktapur. Nepal's cultural heart, seen properly.
items.imageAlt: Kathmandu Durbar Square Nepal UNESCO temple

items.title: Nagarkot Himalayan Sunrise
items.body: A night on the Nagarkot ridge and a pre-dawn wake-up for the moment the 200-kilometre Himalayan arc — eight of the world's highest peaks — ignites gold in the first light of morning.
items.imageAlt: Nagarkot Himalayan sunrise Nepal mountain views

items.title: Pokhara and Phewa Lake
items.body: The Annapurna range reflected in the still water of Phewa Lake, a boat ride to the World Peace Stupa, and Sarangkot at dawn for one of travel's great sunrise moments — all within reach of the most relaxed city in Nepal.
items.imageAlt: Phewa Lake Pokhara Nepal Machapuchare reflection

days.title: Arrive Kathmandu
days.description: Arrive at Tribhuvan International Airport and transfer to your Kathmandu hotel. Evening at leisure in Thamel.

days.title: Kathmandu UNESCO Temples
days.description: Full day with a private guide through Kathmandu's four UNESCO World Heritage Sites — Pashupatinath, Boudhanath, Swayambhunath and Durbar Square.

days.title: Bhaktapur & Nagarkot Overnight
days.description: Morning in medieval Bhaktapur, then drive up to the Nagarkot ridge for sunset and overnight.

days.title: Nagarkot Sunrise & Fly to Pokhara
days.description: Pre-dawn Himalayan sunrise at Nagarkot, then drive or fly to Pokhara. Afternoon at leisure by Phewa Lake.
suggestedActivities: Transfer to Pokhara (25-min flight or 6-hr scenic drive)

days.title: Pokhara: Sarangkot Sunrise & Lakeside
days.description: Early drive to Sarangkot for the Annapurna sunrise, then a full day exploring Pokhara's highlights.

days.title: Depart Pokhara or Kathmandu
days.description: Transfer to Pokhara Airport or return to Kathmandu for your international departure.

whyBook.heading: Nepal's Classic Circuit, Arranged Right
whyBook.body: The Kathmandu–Nagarkot–Pokhara circuit is Nepal's most popular route, which means it needs to be done well to do justice to the country. The right lodges, the right timing for Sarangkot and Nagarkot, and guides who know the temples and the lake. Dhesu has been arranging Himalayan journeys since 1988.

sections.heading: Best of Nepal Tour Package from Malaysia — 6 Days
sections.body:
The Kathmandu–Nagarkot–Pokhara route is Nepal's most-requested itinerary, and for good reason. It combines the cultural capital of Kathmandu, the mountain drama of Nagarkot, and the serene lakeside beauty of Pokhara into one seamless journey.

For Malaysian travellers, six days is the sweet spot — enough to do all three properly without feeling rushed, and achievable in a typical annual leave allocation. Flights between Kuala Lumpur and Kathmandu take around five to six hours.

sections.heading: Phewa Lake & the Sarangkot Sunrise
sections.body:
Pokhara's Phewa Lake is the image most people associate with Nepal — the still water, the Annapurna range reflected in it, and a wooden boat drifting across the blue. The World Peace Stupa on the hill above is an hour's boat ride and a short climb.

Sarangkot, a hill above Pokhara, is Nepal's most famous sunrise viewpoint after Nagarkot. The Annapurna massif fills the horizon — Machapuchare (Fishtail Mountain), Annapurna I, II, III and IV — turning from grey to gold to brilliant white as the sun rises. Plan for the drive up in darkness — it is worth every early morning.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for Nepal?
items.answer: Malaysian passport holders obtain a Visa on Arrival at Kathmandu's Tribhuvan International Airport. A 15-day tourist visa costs USD 30. Bring passport photos and the fee in cash. Dhesu provides full guidance with your booking.

items.question: How do we travel between Kathmandu and Pokhara?
items.answer: The most popular option is a 25-minute domestic flight between Kathmandu and Pokhara, which we include or arrange. The overland route takes approximately 6 to 7 hours by private car through scenic mountain scenery and is a relaxing alternative for those who prefer to see the countryside.

items.question: Which is better — Sarangkot or Nagarkot for sunrise?
items.answer: Both are spectacular and different. Nagarkot (near Kathmandu) offers a wider Himalayan panorama including Everest. Sarangkot (near Pokhara) offers the dramatic close-up of the Annapurna massif and Machapuchare. This package includes both — one of the main reasons it is our most popular Nepal itinerary.

items.question: Is Pokhara good for families?
items.answer: Yes, Pokhara is one of Nepal's most family-friendly destinations. The lakeside is flat, calm and walkable. Boat rides on Phewa Lake are suitable for all ages. The World Peace Stupa hike is moderate and achievable by older children. The pace here is relaxed compared to Kathmandu.

items.question: Can I add Chitwan National Park to this itinerary?
items.answer: Yes. Our 8-day Nepal + Chitwan National Park Wildlife Safari adds Chitwan's jungle safaris, rhino spotting and Rapti River canoe rides to this classic circuit. It is the most complete Nepal experience available.
